title: Chapter 753
content:
Capítulo 753: Is the payoff over? (R-18)

Strax was exhausted.

It wasn’t a simple tiredness, the kind that can be resolved with a night’s sleep. It was the accumulated weight of weeks carrying too many responsibilities at once—Mercedes’ kingdom, decisions that never seemed to end, the constant vigilance against dangers that insisted on appearing where they shouldn’t… and, of course, the far north.

Thirty nights.

Almost without exception.

Scarlet wouldn’t accept rest, and Strax, stubborn as always, hadn’t known how to say no either.

Added to that was the hurried return, the unfinished conversations, Vex’s awakening, the silent tension that now hung between people he loved. Going home should mean relief—but even that seemed to demand too much energy.

“Ah…” The sound escaped him before he even realized he was waking up.

His vision slowly adjusted to the soft light of the room, focusing on the familiar ceiling. For a moment, he stood there, motionless, just breathing, feeling his own body too heavy to move.

Then he realized. The warmth.

The gentle pressure on his lap.

Strax blinked, confused, and lowered his gaze.

Kali was sitting on top of him.

Her tanned skin contrasted with the light fabric of her clothes, her blonde hair falling loosely over her shoulders. She watched him from above, her expression too calm for someone who had clearly taken advantage of the fact that he was asleep.

Her weight wasn’t uncomfortable.

It was… familiar.

Reassuring.

Strax let out a slow sigh, closing his eyes for a brief second before opening them again.

“…good morning,” he murmured, his voice hoarse with sleep and tiredness.

If he was too tired to react, Kali had certainly noticed.

Kali moved slowly, as if aware of his every breath.

She slid forward, lying down on Strax’s body, carefully supporting her weight so as not to disturb him. Her warmth enveloped him immediately, familiar, comforting. One arm rested beside his head, the other on his chest, feeling the still slow rhythm of his heart.

“Good morning…” she murmured, her voice low, almost a whisper.

Strax blinked a few times, still half-lost between sleep and wakefulness, before focusing on her face so close to his. The scent of Kali’s hair, mixed with the warmth of her skin, brought him back to reality.

She tilted her face and began to kiss his neck softly, slow, lazy kisses, more affectionate than provocative. It was the kind of gesture that said “you are home” more than any words.

Strax let out a long sigh.

“You have no idea how dangerous this is for someone who just woke up,” he murmured, with a tired half-smile.

Kali laughed softly, resting her forehead against his for a moment.

“I do.” She gave him another light kiss below his ear. “But I also know when someone needs to be reminded that they can rest.”

He slowly raised his hand, still heavy with sleep, and rested it on her back, pulling her a little closer.

“Then stay like this just a little longer,” he asked. “No wars, no dragons, no kingdoms.”

Kali smiled, closing her eyes.

“Just the two of us,” she replied, before giving him another quiet kiss on his neck, like a true good morning.

Kali didn’t respond immediately.

She just smiled in that slow, overly conscious way, as if she knew exactly the effect she was having.

Her lips returned to Strax’s neck, but now with more intention. Longer kisses, marked by the warmth of their skin, rising and falling in a provocative cadence. She brushed her mouth against his ear, breathing there on purpose, before giving a light bite—nothing that hurt, just enough to elicit a reaction.

Strax took a deep breath, his body finally truly waking up.

“…you’re quite naughty today,” he murmured, his voice still hoarse, but now laden with something more awakened.

Kali let out a low, satisfied laugh and rested her chin on his shoulder, her hair falling like a golden curtain.

“Naughty?” she repeated, feigning innocence. “I’d say neglected.”

She kissed his neck again, higher up, then gently bit his ear, tugging it just slightly before whispering:

“Thirty days, Strax. Thirty. Days.”

He closed his eyes for a moment, as if being judged by an invisible court.

“I was dealing with important things…”

“Ah, I know,” Kali interrupted, pulling back just enough to look at him. Her eyes were gleaming, but also had something deeper there. “Kingdoms, problems, dragons, the end of the world… and that redhead hussy.”

Strax opened his eyes instantly.

“Kali—”

“No.” She placed a finger on his lips, preventing him from answering. “I’m not angry.” A pause. “Not at all.”

She sighed, resting her forehead against his.

“But I’m your wife too. And I stayed here. Waiting. Taking care of everything. Missing you.”

The provocative tone gave way to something more honest, more vulnerable.

Strax raised his hand and pulled her a little closer, the gesture slow, careful.

“I know,” he said softly. “And I’m sorry about that.”

Kali watched his face for a few seconds, as if assessing whether that answer was enough.

Then she smiled again.

“Good.” She settled herself on top of him, comfortable. “So now you’ll have to make it up to me.”

“Now?” he asked, with a tired half-smile.

“Now,” she replied.

Strax raised an eyebrow, the weariness still present in his eyes, but now mixed with a slow fire that Kali knew how to ignite like no one else. He slid his hands down her back, feeling the soft curve beneath the thin fabric of her clothes, and gently pressed her hips against his.

“Now, is it?” he murmured, his husky voice taking on a deeper, more provocative tone. “You’re demanding when you want to be.”
